title: Loops should avoid containing more than a single break or continue statement
description: Loops should not contain more than a single break or continue statement. The use of break and continue statements increases the complexity of the control flow and makes it harder to understand the program logic. In order to keep a good program structure, they should not be applied more than once per loop. The code should be refactored to increase readability if there is more than one break or continue statement.
recommendation: Refactor the loop to use a single break or continue statement, or restructure the logic to avoid multiple exit points.

noncompliant: for (let i = 1; i <= 10; i++) { if (i % 2 == 0) { continue; } if (i % 3 == 0) { continue; } console.log(i); }
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) { if (condition1) { break; } if (condition2) { break; } process(items[i]); }
while (running) { if (check1()) { break; } if (check2()) { continue; } if (check3()) { break; } doWork(); }
compliant: for (let i = 1; i <= 10; i++) { if (i % 2 == 0 || i % 3 == 0) { continue; } console.log(i); }
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) { if (condition1 || condition2) { break; } process(items[i]); }

checks.message: Loop contains more than one break statement - consider refactoring for better readability
Loop contains more than one continue statement - consider refactoring for better readability
